laserPower.description: Optimal average power for ceramic cleaning

spotSize.description: Typical beam diameter for cleaning; 200 μm recommended for BN to provide fine control on detailed surfaces without excessive energy concentration. Fine detail: 100-200 μm; large area: 300-500 μm. Source: Equipment specs from Coherent and Trumpf laser systems for industrial cleaning.

frequency.description: Typical for pulsed cleaning; 30 kHz recommended for BN to balance speed and prevent heat buildup, as higher rates increase average power and risk substrate damage. Trade-off: High freq accelerates cleaning but accumulates heat in low-absorptivity materials like BN at 1064 nm. Source: Academic literature in Journal of Applied Physics on pulse train effects.

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for industrial cleaning to ablate contaminants with minimal HAZ; 20 ns recommended for BN to leverage its thermal conductivity and reduce heat accumulation. Picosecond (ps) or femtosecond for ultra-precision but less common industrially. Source: Laser physics reviews in Optics & Lasers in Engineering (e.g., studies on ceramic ablation).

scanSpeed.description: Effective range for contamination removal; recommended 1000 mm/s for optimal balance of cleaning efficiency and heat control on BN. Light contamination: 1500-3000 mm/s; heavy: 100-500 mm/s. Source: Industry standards from Laserax and Adapt Laser manuals.

passCount.description: Typical for complete removal in multi-pass to ensure uniform cleaning without single-pass overload; 2 passes recommended for BN contamination to minimize risk to substrate integrity. Single pass for light layers. Source: Industry case studies in laser cleaning of ceramics (e.g., SPIE proceedings).

overlapRatio.description: Standard for uniform coverage; 50% recommended for BN to achieve even cleaning while avoiding overexposure and heat zones. Range adjusts for contamination density. Source: Process optimization in Journal of Laser Applications and manufacturer guidelines (e.g., CleanLaser).
